rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture when inserting content: do not change component types, implementations, features, subcomponents, connections, or properties.
Place implementation-level content only in implementation scope, after the relevant structural declarations it depends on.
Respect owner scope: implementation content may reference implementation-local structure, while type-level content must not depend on implementation-only paths.
generic_rules: Keep the insertion minimal and confined to the intended owning declaration.
Do not introduce new architectural elements or rename existing ones during insertion.
Use only identifiers that are already visible in the chosen owner scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AADL content must remain in a valid annex block attached to the correct owner.
Implementation-scoped references typically depend on already-declared subcomponents and connections, so preserve declaration order context.
anti_patterns: Do not move implementation-specific content into a component type owner.
Do not invent missing identifiers, properties, ports, or data declarations to make references resolve.
Do not alter existing routing or structural declarations unless the task explicitly requires architecture edits.
irrelevant_topics: Detailed AGREE clause forms such as assume/guarantee/assign syntax are not needed for this fusion step.
Boolean expression modeling patterns are not needed for owner/scope insertion decisions.
Validator-driven deletion or rewrite strategies are not relevant without active diagnostics.
